label: Three instrumentation approaches for resource-load capture: (1) CLI output parse — regex-match Read()/Glob() calls from dispatched stdout, zero changes to existing binaries; (2) tool-proxy wrapper — spawn child process with structured trace collection; (3) OpenCode MCP intercept — capture tool_use blocks at the transport layer. Approach 1 is recommended as the default, layered with Approach 3 for high-fidelity mode.

The expected-resource key is derived from the skills own RESOURCE_MAP + INTENT_SIGNALS + RUNTIME_ASSETS (canonical declarations from SKILL.md §2), stored in a scenario JSON file NEVER included in the prompt, and loaded only by the post-run scoring pipeline for set-membership comparison against the captured tool-call trace.

Contamination prevention uses a 5-category taxonomy: skill name, trigger phrases, resource paths, intent keywords, and command names — all must be absent from the prompt. A pre-dispatch grep gate (contamination-check.cjs) rejects any scenario where the prompt matches any term in the skills contamination dictionary.

The clean prompt construction protocol builds prompts from domain_description (the real-world problem in domain-generic terms) — never from expected_intent, expected_resources, or any skill-specific term. The APE workflow (Zhou et al. 2022, generate-instruction-from-demonstrations, score-against-held-out-truth) provides the closest prior-art analog to hint-free dispatch.

The end-to-end harness flow has 6 stages with one hard invariant: the expected-resource key NEVER crosses the dispatch boundary (stages 1-2 to 3). The scoring pipeline loads the key independently in stage 5, and contamination is a hard pre-dispatch gate in stage 2. Negative scenarios (irrelevance detection, adapted from BFCL v4) must be included in every batch.

The expected-resource key uses set membership (not ordered list) for scoring — the AI can discover resources in any order. This accommodates the iteration-001 finding that skills do not have deterministic correct chains of resource loads; the same task can be solved by reading references in different orders.

Toolformer (Schick et al. 2023) self-supervised API-call annotation pipeline maps to Lane C trace capture — special tokens demarcate API calls at each generation step, and the filter-by-usefulness step (L+_i vs L-_i) maps to the D4 skill-on/skill-off comparison question: did the loaded resource actually help produce better output?

Trace depth vs breadth: two distinct metrics emerge — initialActivationLatency (tool-calls to first skill resource, measures time-to-activation) vs fullResourceCoverage (all skill resources eventually loaded, measures depth of exploration). Both are useful and should be reported separately rather than collapsed into one metric.
